summary: Pre-open workspace terminal PTYs during startup agent readiness checks, then launch the selected agent after the startup gate clears.

review_items.summary: Authenticated paid workspace rendering mounts under the startup overlay so terminal panes can pre-create PTY pairs without starting a visible bootstrap shell while agent checks or updates are still blocking visible entry.

WorkspaceTerminal can defer agent CLI initialization until the startup gate is complete, then issue one backend batch command that starts the selected agent in all prepared PTYs before the workspace reveal.
The Tauri host exposes a bounded terminal_start_agent_many command that validates panes/providers and spawns agent processes into already-prepared PTYs in one batch.

Frontend and native resize handling includes client sequence and queue timing fields, native resize batches skip stale out-of-order sequences, workspace terminals default to the stable DOM renderer, and release/idle probes capture paint state, DOM row visibility, topmost elements, xterm render events, and layout dimensions to diagnose remaining black flashes.

When workspace terminal settings remove pane indexes, the frontend explicitly closes the corresponding native terminal sessions instead of relying only on React unmount cleanup, and native single-pane close requests log start/done/error telemetry.
The authenticated dashboard still renders only for authenticated paid users; free users and signed-out users cannot pre-open workspace terminals.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, or data access change is required.

patches.summary: Mount the authenticated dashboard behind the startup overlay, add terminal prewarm/deferred agent launch props, explicitly close removed terminal panes, keep workspace terminals on the DOM renderer during resize, and add resize release/idle paint diagnostics.
Support shell-only terminal_open requests, add terminal_start_agent for launching an agent inside the prewarmed PTY, and log single-pane terminal_close outcomes.

compatibility.notes: This changes only local desktop startup sequencing. Existing auth, billing, workspace API, CLI install/update, and terminal write/resize/close contracts are preserved.

tests.summary: Validate the Rust Tauri command changes.
